description: Fix a rate-limit retry parsing bug in the Slack plugin's API client that causes NaN to be passed as retryAfterMs to ToolError.rateLimited
qualityChecks: cd plugins/slack && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix NaN retryAfterMs when Slack 429 response has no Retry-After header
userStories.description: As an AI agent using the Slack plugin, I want rate-limit errors to include a valid retryAfterMs value so that I can implement correct retry backoff instead of receiving NaN
acceptanceCriteria: When slackApi receives a 429 response with no Retry-After header, ToolError.rateLimited is called with a numeric retryAfterMs (not NaN) — either a sensible default like 60000 or undefined
When slackApi receives a 429 response with a valid Retry-After header (e.g., '30'), ToolError.rateLimited is called with retryAfterMs = 30000
When slackApi receives a 429 response with a non-numeric Retry-After header (e.g., an HTTP-date), ToolError.rateLimited is called with either a correctly parsed ms value or undefined — never NaN
cd plugins/slack && npm run build passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run format:check passes
